 {"id":"bd-12o8.6","title":"Advanced Text Editor — UX/A11y Review","description":"UX/a11y review for Advanced Text Editor: keybindings, focus order, and contrast/legibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Advanced Text Editor — UX/A11y Review
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-12o8.7","title":"Advanced Text Editor: Rope Invariants + Amortized Proof","description":"Formalize rope balance invariants and amortized O(log n) edit bounds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Advanced Text Editor: Rope Invariants + Amortized Proof
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Math spec: define the model, priors/assumptions, and failure modes; include a reproducible notebook-like derivation in comments/docs
- Guarantees: prefer anytime-valid testing (e-process) or distribution-free calibration (conformal) where applicable

 {"id":"bd-12o8.8","title":"Advanced Text Editor: Search/Replace Optimization","description":"Optimize search/replace with automata (Aho-Corasick) and profiling.,

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Advanced Text Editor: Search/Replace Optimization
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema

Deliverables

 {"id":"bd-130l","title":"Responsive Layout Breakpoints System","description":"## Background

Terminals vary wildly in size:
- Phone SSH client: 40x20
- Standard terminal: 80x24
- Modern laptop: 120x40
- 4K monitor: 200x60+

Currently, widgets either overflow or waste space. Dashboard could show 1 column on small, 3 columns on large.

## Solution: Responsive Layout System

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Breakpoint {
    XSmall,  // < 60 cols
    Small,   // 60-79 cols
    Medium,  // 80-119 cols
    Large,   // 120-159 cols
    XLarge,  // 160+ cols
}

impl Breakpoint {
    pub fn from_width(width: u16) -> Self {
        match width {
            0..60 => Self::XSmall,
            60..80 => Self::Small,
            80..120 => Self::Medium,
            120..160 => Self::Large,
            _ => Self::XLarge,
        }
    }
    
    pub fn is_at_least(&self, other: Self) -> bool;
    pub fn is_at_most(&self, other: Self) -> bool;
}

/// Responsive value that changes with breakpoint
pub struct Responsive<T> {
    xsmall: T,
    small: Option<T>,
    medium: Option<T>,
    large: Option<T>,
    xlarge: Option<T>,
}

impl<T: Clone> Responsive<T> {
    pub fn get(&self, bp: Breakpoint) -> &T;
}

// Usage
let columns = Responsive::new(1)  // XSmall: 1 column
    .medium(2)                     // Medium: 2 columns
    .large(3);                     // Large: 3 columns

let layout = ResponsiveLayout::new()
    .xsmall(|area| Layout::vertical([...]))
    .medium(|area| Layout::horizontal([...]));
```

## Integration with Layout Crate

```rust
// In ftui-layout
pub struct Layout {
    // ... existing fields
    responsive: Option<Box<dyn Fn(Breakpoint, Rect) -> Layout>>,
}

impl Layout {
    pub fn responsive<F>(f: F) -> Self
    where
        F: Fn(Breakpoint, Rect) -> Layout + 'static;
}
```

## Benefits

- Optimal UX on any terminal size
- CSS-like mental model
- Progressive enhancement

## Files to Create

- crates/ftui-layout/src/responsive.rs
- crates/ftui-layout/src/breakpoint.rs

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Responsive Layout Breakpoints System
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests
- Formal invariants documented + enforced via property tests; any dynamic decisions have an evidence ledger and debug UI


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.
- Layout: formulate constraints explicitly; consider LP/dual sensitivity for responsive variants; enforce stable tie-breaking for determinism.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.
- Metamorphic tests: shrink/grow, reorder non-semantic inputs, and ensure invariants still hold.

 {"id":"bd-130l.1","title":"Breakpoint Configuration System","description":"Define breakpoint thresholds for responsive layouts.

## Requirements
- Named breakpoints (xs, sm, md, lg, xl)
- Column-based and pixel-based thresholds
- Custom breakpoint definitions
- Query API for current breakpoint

## Implementation
- Breakpoint enum with threshold values
- BreakpointConfig in theme or app config
- current_breakpoint(width) function

## Tests
- Threshold boundary testing
- Custom breakpoint registration
- Query accuracy

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Breakpoint Configuration System
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-130l.2","title":"Responsive Style Variants","description":"Apply different styles based on current breakpoint.

## Requirements
- Style variants per breakpoint
- Inheritance from smaller breakpoints
- Runtime style switching
- No layout recalculation if style-only change

## Implementation
- ResponsiveStyle wrapper
- at_breakpoint(bp, style) builder
- Efficient lookup in style resolution

## Tests
- Style switching at boundaries
- Inheritance chain
- Performance of style lookup

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Style Variants
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
